items.hook: ‘He made wine and he shared it. What more do you want?’: Sam Neill remembered by his co-stars
items.body: The actors Lindsay Duncan and Charles Dance, alongside director Peter Webber, pay tribute to a practical joker, unpretentious craftsman and ‘very cool guy’

items.hook: The Finnish War: the Battle of Lapua is fought.
items.body: The Finnish War was fought between the Kingdom of Sweden and the Russian Empire from 21 February 1808 to 17 September 1809 as part of the Napoleonic Wars. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The first ascent of the Matterhorn is completed by Edward Whymper and his party, four of whom die on the descent.
items.body: The first ascent of the Matterhorn was a mountaineering expedition of the Matterhorn made by Edward Whymper, Lord Francis Douglas, Charles Hudson, Douglas Hadow, Michel Croz, and two Zermatt guides, Peter Taugwalder and his son of the same name, on 14 July 1865. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Little Rot is a 2024 novel written by Nigerian writer Akwaeke Emezi.
items.body: This novel follows five friends who become ensnared in a mess of sex, lies, and corruption. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: I Wouldn't Be in Your Shoes is a 1943 collection of five novellas by American crime writer Cornell Woolrich under the pseudonym "William Irish".
items.body: It includes one of Woolrich's most noted works, Nightmare. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Opening of the first major US world's fair, the Exhibition of the Industry of All Nations in New York City.
items.body: A world's fair, also known as a universal exhibition, is a large global exhibition designed to showcase the achievements of nations. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Wonderland is a Japanese manga series written and illustrated by Yūgo Ishikawa.
items.body: It was serialized in Shogakukan's seinen manga magazine Big Comic Superior from April 2015 to October 2017. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: 'I saw off 300,000 competitors to become a Cheetah Girl'
items.body: The Cheetah Girls are making a comeback with a new generation of young stars.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Originally released in the United States in 1970, it was published in Germany the next year, as Holt Hartmann vom Himmel!.
items.body: The Blond Knight of Germany is a book by the American authors Trevor J. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Ladies First has been panned. It’s still an essential watch | Anna Smith
items.body:
The Netflix gender-flip comedy film, in which Sacha Baron Cohen wakes to a world run by female chauvinists, is a valuable testimony to the enduring destructive force of gender bias

‘This Movie Is: Absurd.” That’s the descriptive la

items.hook: YouTube still recommending eating disorder videos to teens, research finds
items.body: The site is continuing to recommend harmful videos despite new rules meant to better protect teenagers in the UK, a study finds.

items.hook: Sam Neill’s final interview: ‘I’d like to think that, in life, I’m a goodie’
items.body: The actor, who has died aged 78, recently answered

items.hook: Andy Serkis defends lack of diversity in Lord of the Rings cast
items.body: Kate Winslet, Jamie Dornan and Anya Taylor-Joy are among the stars who have been cast so far.

items.hook: To the north is Soho Square and to the south is Shaftesbury Avenue.
items.body: Frith Street is in the Soho area of London. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Harry Atwood, an exhibition pilot for the Wright brothers, is greeted by president William Howard Taft after he lands his aeroplane on the South Lawn of the White House, having flown from Boston.
items.body: Harry Nelson Atwood was an American engineer and inventor known for pioneering work in the early days of aviation, including setting long-distance flying records and delivering the first air mail in New England. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Blackford "Blackie"/"Black" Oakes is a fictional character, a Central Intelligence Agency officer, spy and the protagonist of a series of novels written by William F.
items.body: The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Rus Flight 9633 crashes during takeoff from Chkalovsky Airport, killing all 10 people on board.
items.body: Rus Flight 9633 refers to the crash involving an IL-76TD aircraft operated by Rus Airlines from Chkalovsky Airport to Taiyuan Wusu Airport with intermediate stops at Alykel Airport and Bratsk Airport. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Joan of Arc, taken by the Burgundians in May, is handed over to Pierre Cauchon, the bishop of Beauvais.
items.body: Joan of Arc is a patron saint of France, honored as a defender of the French nation for her role in the siege of Orléans and her insistence on the coronation of Charles VII of France during the Hundred Years' War. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: I interviewed Sam Neill in 2024. He was even more charming than I’d expected | Zoe Williams
items.body: The actor, who has died aged 78, was refreshingly candid about Hollywood, filmmaking, his cancer treatment – and why he preferred life on his New Zealand farm to the movie business

items.hook: Nebraska is a 1987 gay novel by American author George Whitmore.
items.body: It is a coming of age story about Craig McMullen, a boy in Nebraska who lost his leg in a car accident, and the development of his sexual identity. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.
